LanguageData.PawnDiary.Command.OpenDiaryDesc: Open or close this pawn's diary.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Tab.NoEntries: No pages have been written yet.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Tab.GenerateForPawn: Write diary pages for this pawn
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Tab.GenerateForPawnTip: When disabled, events are still remembered here, but no new diary page is sent to the writer for this pawn.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Tab.ShowPersonaSettingsTip: Show the manual writing-style picker in this pawn's Diary tab.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Tab.ShowLlmDebugInfoTip: Show raw, pending, and failed entries with endpoint, model, status, error, and prompt diagnostics.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Tab.ShowGeneratingEntriesTip: Reveal pages still being written or stuck on "writing..." so you can see which events never finished. Lighter than the full LLM debug view.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Tab.FillMockEntriesTip: Dev-only: adds completed mock pages across many in-game dates so long-history scrolling, year paging, and collapse behavior can be tested without calling an LLM.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Tab.MockEntriesAlreadyFilled: {1}'s diary already has {0} mock test pages.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Tab.GeneratePromptSuiteTip: Dev-only: turns on prompt test mode and opens a dropdown of event categories. Pick one to capture a single prompt-only page for that category (plain, no decoration); picking another replaces it. Pair categories need a second colonist.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Tab.ClearPromptSuiteTip: Dev-only: delete every prompt-test entry from all colonists' diaries.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Tab.PromptSuiteEmpty: No prompt test pages were captured for {0}. Enable diary generation for this pawn and try again.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PromptSuite.Instruction: write a short diary entry for this synthetic dev test event
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PromptSuite.Insult.Label: Insult
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PromptSuite.Romance.Label: Married
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PromptSuite.MentalBreak.Te: {0} snapped and flew into a berserk rage.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PromptSuite.Hediff.Label: Fell ill
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PromptSuite.Work.Label: Research
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PromptSuite.Thought.Label: Ate without a table
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PromptSuite.Thought.Text: {0} ate a meal off the ground.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PromptSuite.MoodEvent.Text: A blistering heat wave settled over the colony.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PromptSuite.Tale.Label: Finished research
Today brought focused research, a stinging insult, and a creeping fever.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Tab.DevPreviewTip: Dev-only: shows one temporary diary page at the top of the list so text wrapping, markdown, linked cards, writing/title animations, colors, speech blocks, and atmosphere decorations can be checked without saving mock events.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Tab.LinkedTooltip: Click to view {0}'s diary entry for this event
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Tab.YearSelectorTip: Click to choose a diary year.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Tab.ExpandCollapseTip: Click the header to expand or collapse this page.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Tab.CopyEntryTip: Dev-only: copy this page's text to the clipboard. Prompt-only cards copy the captured prompt; other cards copy the generated text.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Error.SkippedIncapacitated: Skipped because the pawn was below {0}% Consciousness when generation would have started.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Error.PromptTestModeCaptured: Prompt test mode captured this prompt without sending it to an LLM.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.ApisHeader: APIs — enabled rows run in parallel (one model each; add more rows for more models)
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.CustomAuthHeaderTip: HTTP header name for the API key, such as x-goog-api-key, api-key, or x-api-key.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.ConnectionTestMissing: Connection test needs an endpoint URL.
Connection test needs a model name.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.ConnectionTestPrompt: Reply with one short sentence confirming the API connection works.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.GenerateTitles: Write short titles for diary pages
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.GenerateTitlesTip: When enabled, each diary entry sends a small follow-up LLM request to write a short title after the main page is written. Adds latency and one extra token bill per entry. When disabled, no new title is written and diary headers show only the date.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.EnableAtmosphericForm: When enabled, only extreme diary pages can use unusual spacing, staggered variable-size words, or dramatic strange-chat speech distortion. Mental breaks, anomaly/dark entries, death descriptions, and pages written while heavily impaired may be affected. Prompts and saved diary text are unchanged.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.EnablePromptEnchantme: When enabled, first-person diary prompts may include one important live context cue. XML controls which health conditions and important-event status cues can appear and how often they win the weighted random pick.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.InjectGeneratedSpeech: Add generated speech to the Social log

When enabled, a completed initiator diary entry with a valid speech block adds one new Social-log line containing that generated spoken text. This can let mods that watch new interaction log entries react after the LLM result is ready.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.PromptTestMode: Prompt test mode: capture prompts only
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.PromptTestModeTip: Dev-only: real gameplay events assemble and save their system and user prompts, but no diary or title request is sent to an LLM. Prompt-only cards appear in the Diary tab while dev mode is on.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.TemperatureHelp: Controls model randomness for diary and title requests. Lower values are steadier and more literal; higher values are more varied and atmospheric but can drift from supplied facts.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.TimeoutSecondsHelp: Maximum seconds to wait for one API lane before failing over or marking the request failed. Slow local models may need more than the default 30 seconds.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.MaxTokensHelp: Upper token cap for diary responses before local sentence-aware cleanup. Higher values allow longer pages but slow generation and can make entries less concise.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.WorkGenerationWeightH: Multiplies the random chance that periodic work sampling becomes a diary page. 1x uses the XML tuning defaults; 0x suppresses random work pages.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.SocialGenerationWeigh: Multiplies the random chance that batched low-stakes social moments are promoted into immediate pairwise diary pages. 1x uses the XML tuning defaults; 0x keeps them batched.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.MaxConcurrentHelp: How many requests may be in flight at once on each API; the rest wait in that API's queue. Different APIs always run in parallel, so adding more APIs above speeds up bursts. Use 1 for a single local model that handles one request at a time; raise it only if an endpoint serves requests in parallel.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.PromptStudioHelp: Edit shared system prompts and per-event prompt guidance. Prompt templates, final instructions, and event filters stay in XML defs.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.PromptStudioSummary: {0} XML prompt templates and {1} event prompt types loaded; {2} event types customized in settings
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.PromptStudioXmlNote: Field lists, event filters, group instructions, and writing-style catalog defaults are still edited in XML, then applied after restarting RimWorld.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.PersonaStudioHelp: Edit writing-style labels, style rules, and theme tags from DiaryPersonaDefs.xml, or add your own. Tags drive the first-roll weighting for new pawns.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Settings.PersonaStudioTagsHelp: Custom writing styles must keep at least one predefined tag.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.SocialFight: {0} and {1} lost their tempers and came to blows.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.BatchInstruction: write one diary entry for the overall {0} exchange, not separate entries for each listed moment
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.TaleBatchInstruction: write one solo diary entry for the overall {0} stretch, not separate entries for each listed moment
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.CombatBatchBrief: A dangerous combat moment left a mark.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.CombatBatchFallback: {0} lived through a hard stretch of combat.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.CombatBatchInstruction: write one solo diary entry about the overall fight, using the listed combat log lines as factual evidence; do not report each wound, downing, or kill separately
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.InsultBatchInstruction: write one diary entry for the overall conflict, using the listed game log lines as factual evidence; do not report each insult separately
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.AmbientDayFallback: {0} had a few quiet {1} moments today.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.AmbientDayInstruction: write one natural diary paragraph about the background texture of the day; do not summarize it as a log, do not list every moment, do not mention counts, and use names only when they feel emotionally relevant
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.AmbientSocialHeader: Passing social moments colored the day:
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.AmbientSocialFallback: {0} drifted through a few small social moments today.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.AmbientSocialInstruction: write one natural diary paragraph about the social atmosphere of the day; treat the listed conversations as background evidence, not events to report; do not list names or counts unless a name carries emotional weight
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.AmbientStrangeChatFallba: {0} carried the aftertaste of strange conversations today.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.AmbientStrangeChatInstru: write one natural diary paragraph about the unease these strange conversations left behind; treat the listed chats as background evidence, not separate reports; keep the wrongness unresolved
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.AmbientAnimalFallback: {0} spent part of the day around the colony's animals.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.AmbientAnimalInstruction: write one natural diary paragraph about the feel of working around animals today; do not turn the moments into a training log, and only name animals or handlers if it feels personal
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.AmbientTeachingHeader: Teaching and learning moments colored the day:
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.AmbientTeachingFallback: {0} spent part of the day around teaching and learning.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.AmbientTeachingInstructi: write one natural diary paragraph about the feeling of teaching or learning today; avoid lesson-by-lesson reporting and focus on the relationship or mood it left behind
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.AmbientThoughtHeader: Small feelings and impressions colored the day:
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.AmbientThoughtFallback: {0} carried a few passing feelings through the day.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.AmbientThoughtInstructio: write one natural diary paragraph about the emotional texture these passing feelings left behind; do not list each thought, do not mention counts, and only name the source of a feeling if it would matter to the pawn
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Prompt.PairDirectSpeechInstruc: Initiator POV for {0}: when the notes contain, report, or strongly imply spoken words from {0}, include exactly one short line on its own as [[speech]]...[[/speech]]. Put only {0}'s spoken words inside; no narration. Example: I told {1} to stop. [[speech]]Enough.[[/speech]] Wrong: tagging {1}'s reply, tagging thoughts, or inventing speech. If {0} did not speak, use no speech block and write {0}'s private reaction instead.

Recipient POV for {0}: do not use [[speech]] blocks in this second-perspective entry. Paraphrase any spoken words as {0}'s private reaction to {1}; do not quote {1}, do not tag thoughts as speech, and do not invent words for {0} to say.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Prompt.SoloInteractionDirectSp: Single-POV interaction for {0}: when the notes contain, report, or strongly imply spoken words from {0}, include exactly one short line on its own as [[speech]]...[[/speech]]. Put only {0}'s spoken words inside; no narration. Example: I muttered under my breath. [[speech]]Not again.[[/speech]] Wrong: tagging another pawn, tagging thoughts, or inventing speech. If {0} did not speak, use no speech block and write {0}'s private reaction instead.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.DayReflectionHeader: Of everything today, these stayed with {0}:
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.DayReflectionFallback: {0} settled down at the end of a quiet, unremarkable day.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.DayReflectionInstruction: write one short reflective diary paragraph as {0} settling down at day's end, looking back on the day as a whole; weave together only the moments below that would still be on their mind, using their writing style; these are the highlights they already lived, so reflect on how the day felt rather than re-reporting each event; do not write a list or mention counts
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.DayReflectionFillerLine: the ordinary background of small talk and passing moods
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.StartingArrival: {0} was one of the colony's founding colonists.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.RitualFinished: {0} finished the ritual {1} as {2}; ritual role: {3}.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Ritual.Role.Author: author
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.RitualInstruction.Author: write as the ritual's author/organizer after it has finished; carry responsibility, public pressure, and what the completed ceremony cost or proved
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.RitualInstruction.Target: write as the ritual's target after it has finished; center what it meant to be singled out, watched, changed, judged, honored, or used by the ceremony
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.RitualInstruction.Partic: write as a ritual participant after it has finished; use the assigned role and ritual title as concrete pressure, not background decoration
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.RitualInstruction.Specta: write as a spectator after the ritual has finished; focus on watching, judging, belonging, doubt, awe, discomfort, or relief from the edge of the ceremony
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.PsychicRitualInstruction: write as the psychic ritual's invoker after it has completed; make the atmosphere unsettling, scary, dark, or plain incomprehensible; include exactly one short standalone [[speech]]...[[/speech]] block containing invented, broken, or half-understood ritual words from the invoker, and put no narration inside that block
write as the psychic ritual's target after it has completed; make the event frightening, violating, or darkly numbing without explaining the occult mechanism
write as someone involved in the psychic ritual after it has completed; keep the atmosphere unsettling, scary, and hard to fully understand
write as someone who watched the psychic ritual complete; focus on dread, wrongness, fear, silence, strange sound, or relief from the edge of it
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.MockBody: Mock diary page {0}. {1} carried this deliberately artificial memory through the day so the diary can be tested with a long, multi-year history.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PreviewLinkedText: The other perspective preview should sit inside the main card, with its own border, accent strip, label line, and clipped body text.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PreviewBodyPlain:
This is a simulated diary page for {0}. It includes **bold memory**, *uneasy emphasis*, and "quoted words" inside ordinary prose.
The second paragraph is deliberately longer so wrapping, spacing, and the page bottom can be checked without waiting for an LLM.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PreviewBodyMarkdown: # A heading-like line - A bullet marker should become a compact bullet. > A quote marker should disappear while the words remain. This line checks **bold**, *italic*, and "inline spoken words" in the ordinary body renderer.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PreviewBodySpeech: {0} writes a short ordinary line before saying something out loud. [[speech]]This line should become the colored speech block.[[/speech]] After the speech, ordinary narration should return to the diary body style.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PreviewBodyStaggered: {0} tries to keep the page steady after too much drink. [[speech]]I can write this straight. The room keeps tilting, the letters keep slipping, and I am almost sure nobody can tell.[[/speech]] Only the spoken block should pick up the configured staggered word-size decoration.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PreviewBodyCombat:
{0} is testing the combat diary color and a longer, harsher entry shape. The page should carry the combat accent while ordinary narration still wraps cleanly around strong verbs, clipped sentences, and **important** damage details.
A second combat paragraph checks that the card keeps enough bottom padding after a dense account of smoke, blood, shouted orders, and the sick quiet that follows a fight.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PreviewBodySocialFight: {0} is testing the social-fight accent, where the page should feel angry without using the full combat color. Short lines should still stay readable. The card should show the orange conflict cue, ordinary body text, and enough space after the last sentence.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PreviewBodyMental:
{0} is testing the fractured mental-break layout. One thought holds still. Another snaps sideways. The room answers anyway. Hands shake. The margin keeps moving.
The second paragraph checks that fractured spacing still respects blank-line gaps while short broken sentences scatter across the page.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PreviewBodyDark: {0} is testing the extreme-dark cue and unsettled layout. The page should lean into the dark red accent without losing legibility. [[speech]]Something moved where there was no light.[[/speech]] The spoken line should dim selected words instead of using the strange-chat glyph distortion.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PreviewBodyStrange: {0} records the moment as carefully as possible, but the lines do not want to stay where they belong. [[speech]]The walls answered before I spoke.[[/speech]] The strange preview keeps the unsettled layout and the color cue that drives Zalgo-style direct-speech distortion.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PreviewBodyDeath:
{0}'s death-preview page should settle into the centered memorial layout. The words are not a real eulogy; they are only here to check the pale accent, spacing, and quiet line breaks.
What remains is a measured silence, a name held carefully, and enough text to prove the memorial card can breathe without clipping.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PreviewBodyLinked:
{0} is testing the main side of a pairwise diary card. The linked preview above or below should reserve height correctly, stay inside its border, and keep the main text from clipping.
This page also checks that the linked card and body text use separate colors and spacing.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PreviewBodyWriting: {0} is testing the in-progress writing placeholder. This body is stored as raw preview data, but the card should show the animated writing dots instead of finished prose while the status is pending.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Dev.PreviewBodyTitle: {0} is testing the title-pending header. The body is complete, but the header should keep the date visible and animate small dots where the generated title will appear.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.ThoughtProgressionInstru: this is a worsening stage of an ongoing need or situation; write about the current severity, do not treat it as a brand-new unrelated thought, and do not repeat earlier stages
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Event.WorkDarkStudy: {0} studied what should not be studied: {1}.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Error.SkippedInitiatorFailed: Skipped because the initiator diary page failed to write.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Error.NoApiConfigured: No API is configured. Add an endpoint and model in the mod settings.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Error.TitleEmptyResponse: Title follow-up returned no usable text.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Prompt.PersonaVoice: How this pawn tends to write: {0} Follow the rule's concrete sentence shape, opening move, punctuation, and detail choice. Do not roleplay a chat persona, add catchphrases, or invent dialogue.
LanguageData.PawnDiary.Prompt.Context.Cue.RespectIfRe: respect this status if it changes how the pawn would frame the event
